prompt: Using ProteinMPNN, design a panel of SARS-CoV-2 spike RBD sequences (PDB 6M0J) optimised for high-yield recombinant production in HEK293 cells as vaccine antigens, while preserving the ACE2-binding epitopes targeted by broadly neutralising antibodies. Apply sequence design to surface-exposed, non-epitope positions only. outline a computational approach covering: (1) mask definition: fix all ACE2-contacting residues (positions 417, 449, 453, 455–456, 473, 475–476, 486, 487, 489, 493, 496, 498–502, 505), (2) ProteinMPNN sampling (temperature 0.3) with 500 sequences, (3) ESMFold pLDDT and predicted ACE2 binding interface scoring, (4) top-10 sequences for expression and neutralisation antibody binding ELISA. Discuss considerations for N-linked glycosylation site preservation.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and RCSB PDB (6M0J); target: Spike RBD; organism: SARS-CoV-2; catalogue version v1.0
